steps.title: Factor the numerator
steps.content:
The two numerator terms share a factor of $(x-6)$:
$$
7x(x-6)-4(x-6)=(x-6)(7x-4)
$$

steps.title: Factor the denominator
steps.content:
The denominator also contains a factor of $(x-6)$:
$$
3x-18=3(x-6)
$$
Because $x>6$, this factor is not $0$, so canceling it is allowed.

steps.title: Cancel the common factor
steps.content: Cancel the common nonzero factor $(x-6)$: $$ \frac{(x-6)(7x-4)}{3(x-6)}=\frac{7x-4}{3} $$ Choices that keep $(x-6)$ or expand incorrectly do not match this simplified expression. The answer is B.
